# Weekly Meal Planning Strategy

## Budget Target

$150/week ($600/month) for a family of 4 (Nina, Alex, Leo age 10, Mila age 6). This breaks down to approximately $5.36 per person per day, or $1.79 per meal. In practice, breakfasts average $1.00/person, lunches $1.50/person, and dinners $2.50/person, with snacks and beverages absorbing the remainder.

## Sunday Prep Session (2-3 hours)

### Proteins (45 minutes)
- **Roast one whole chicken** (Costco organic, $2.49/lb, ~5 lbs = $12.45): Season with salt, pepper, herbs. Roast at 425F for 1 hour. Yields: dinner Sunday night + shredded chicken for 2-3 more meals (tacos, salads, rice bowls). Save carcass for stock.
- **Marinate tofu**: Press 2 blocks firm tofu (Nasoya organic, $2.99/block from PCC). Marinate in soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, garlic. Bake Monday or Tuesday.
- **Hard boil eggs**: 8 eggs for snacks and salads. Costco organic eggs, $6.49/18 = $0.36/egg.

### Grains (30 minutes)
- **Cook 4 cups dry rice** (basmati or jasmine): Yields approximately 10 cups cooked. Use throughout the week for stir-fries, rice bowls, and sides. Costco Tilda basmati 10-lb bag: $15.99.
- **Cook 2 cups dry quinoa**: For salads and bowls. Bob's Red Mill from PCC, $5.99/lb.

### Vegetables (45 minutes)
- **Wash and dry all leafy greens**: Spin in salad spinner, store in containers lined with paper towels. Extends freshness from 3 days to 7 days.
- **Chop onions**: Dice 4-5 onions, store in airtight container. Used in almost every dinner recipe.
- **Prep snack vegetables**: Cut carrots, celery, bell peppers into sticks. Store in water in the fridge (stays crisp 5-7 days). These are critical for Mila's lunchbox.
- **Roast a sheet pan of vegetables**: Sweet potatoes, broccoli, cauliflower. Toss with olive oil, salt, pepper. Roast at 425F for 25 minutes. Use as side dishes or in grain bowls.

## Sample Weekly Menu

### Monday
- **Breakfast**: Overnight oats (prepped Sunday night — oats, yogurt, chia seeds, berries)
- **Lunch**: Kids: PB&J + apple + carrot sticks. Adults: grain bowl with quinoa, roasted vegetables, chicken, tahini dressing.
- **Dinner**: Chicken tikka masala (see recipe) with basmati rice. Prep time: 35 minutes with pre-marinated chicken.

### Tuesday
- **Breakfast**: Sourdough toast with butter and jam (homemade bread from Saturday bake)
- **Lunch**: Kids: pasta with butter and parmesan. Adults: leftover tikka masala.
- **Dinner**: Baked tofu stir-fry with broccoli, bell pepper, snap peas, rice. Sauce: soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, sesame oil, cornstarch slurry. 25 minutes.

### Wednesday
- **Breakfast**: Smoothies (frozen banana, frozen berries, yogurt, spinach — Leo adds peanut butter)
- **Lunch**: Kids: cheese quesadilla + fruit. Adults: big salad with chicken, avocado, hard-boiled egg.
- **Dinner**: Pasta alla Norma with San Marzano tomato sauce, roasted eggplant, ricotta salata. Sourdough bread on the side. 30 minutes.

### Thursday
- **Breakfast**: Scrambled eggs with toast (Leo helps cook these)
- **Lunch**: Leftovers from previous nights
- **Dinner**: Tacos with shredded chicken, black beans (canned, rinsed), avocado, salsa, cheese, sour cream. Mila's favorite meal — she eats 3 tacos. 20 minutes.

### Friday
- **Breakfast**: Yogurt (homemade, see fermentation.md) with granola and fruit
- **Lunch**: Kids: school hot lunch. Adults: leftovers or cafe lunch.
- **Dinner**: Pizza night. Homemade dough (Caputo 00 flour, 24-hour cold ferment started Thursday night). Toppings: Margherita for adults, cheese only for Mila, pepperoni for Leo. 45 minutes including bake time.

### Saturday
- **Breakfast**: Pancakes or waffles (family tradition — Leo makes the batter)
- **Lunch**: Sandwiches with fresh sourdough from morning bake
- **Dinner**: Flexible — often eat out, or try a new recipe. This is our experimentation night.

### Sunday
- **Breakfast**: Eggs, sourdough toast, fruit
- **Lunch**: Light — soup or salad using whatever needs to be used up
- **Dinner**: Roast chicken with roasted vegetables and rice (kicks off the next week's prep cycle)

## Shopping Strategy

- **Costco (biweekly)**: Chicken, eggs, rice, olive oil, butter, frozen fruit, cheese, tortillas
- **PCC Community Markets (weekly)**: Fresh produce, specialty ingredients, tofu, fish, artisan bread for days between sourdough bakes
- **H Mart (monthly)**: Gochugaru, soy sauce, rice vinegar, noodles, tofu, fresh ginger
- **Online (quarterly)**: Burlap & Barrel spices, King Arthur flour (25-lb bags), Rancho Gordo dried beans
